description: Broad homelab + server suite: hardware probes, systemd services, Docker, journals, Linux errors, reboots, and edge-case phrasing to stress every allowlisted evidence path.

items.question: Is my disk running out of space anywhere on this system?
How full is the root filesystem and should I worry?
Which Docker containers restarted a lot recently and what do the logs say?
Show me errors from my Docker containers in the last few hours.
Are there any 5xx errors from nginx in the logs?
Did Apache httpd log any errors today?
Do the logs show SSH brute-force or repeated failed logins?
Were there failed sudo attempts or permission denials?
What systemd units failed to start recently?
Any kernel oops, panic, or BUG messages recently?
Was the OOM killer invoked and which process was killed?
PostgreSQL errors or connection failures in the logs?
MySQL or MariaDB errors in the last day?
Redis connection or startup errors?
TLS certificate expiry or handshake errors mentioned in logs?
Did any cron jobs fail or produce error output?
Signs that a backup or rsync job failed?
RAID or mdadm events or disk degradation warnings?
ZFS pool or scrub errors in the logs?
Network disconnects or carrier loss events?
DHCP or DNS resolution problems on this host?
Firewall drops or iptables/nftables denials?
apt or dpkg errors during package operations?
dnf/yum package errors if this were a RHEL-style box?
When did the system last reboot and is there a reason in the logs?
Evidence of unexpected shutdown or power loss?
Who logged in to this system recently (successful logins)?
fail2ban bans or jail actions in the logs?
Mail server (postfix/exim) errors or queue problems?
SMTP TLS or STARTTLS failures?
Upstream connection errors behind a reverse proxy?
Let's Encrypt certbot or ACME renewal failures?
Signs of heavy swap use or memory pressure?
Periods of very high load average and what was running?
Disk I/O errors, SCSI/NVMe medium errors, or I/O timeouts?
Filesystem remounted read-only or ext4/btrfs errors?
systemd timers that failed or missed runs?
journal-level err or crit messages in the last 24 hours?
Docker healthcheck failures for any service?
NVIDIA Xid GPU errors or driver resets in logs?
AMD ROCm or GPU-related errors?
CPU thermal throttling or temperature warnings?
NTP or time synchronization problems?
NFS stale file handle or mount errors?
Samba or CIFS authentication or mount errors?
Do logs suggest this host is under attack or being scanned?
What errors appeared right before the last service outage window I care about?
SSL/TLS handshake failures from clients?
Did the database process restart unexpectedly?
Docker build or BuildKit errors in captured logs?
LVM thin pool or volume group errors?
btrfs checksum or space errors?
WireGuard or VPN tunnel errors?
Application crashes or core dumps logged?
SELinux AVC denials or AppArmor denials?
AppArmor DENIED messages?
SMART or disk predictive failure warnings?
USB device reset or disconnect storms?
CUPS or printing subsystem errors?
snapd errors or snap refresh failures?
Flatpak or related errors in user session logs?
rsnapshot or backup rotation errors mentioned in logs?
Is my Plex (or similar) service logging errors?
What failed on this server in the last hour?
Summarize the most important errors from today.
What is the CPU temperature on this machine right now (or closest thermal signal you have)?
Is load average unusually high for the number of CPUs on this host?
Do the logs show memory pressure, swap thrash, or OOM-like signals recently?
Is the disk I/O saturated or the storage subsystem struggling (not just disk full)?
Did any network interface go down/up or lose carrier recently?
What is GPU utilization, temperature, and VRAM use right now (if a GPU exists)?
Give an overview of important running services on this server — especially anything that looks failed or restarting.
Is sshd running and in a healthy state according to live service data and logs?
Is PostgreSQL (the database service) up, and did it fail or restart recently?
Is nginx running as a native service and stable, or flapping?
Is Samba / smbd working properly as a native service (not only Docker logs)?
List systemd units that are in failed state or exited with errors in the last 7 days.
Is the cron / scheduled-jobs daemon healthy on this host?
Summarize Docker on this machine: which containers exist, which are running, and any high RestartCount from live engine data.
Which Docker containers are stopped vs running right now, and anything suspicious?
From Docker container logs only, what errors or fatals stand out in the last 6 hours?
Anything wrong in postgres-related container logs recently?
What does the systemd journal say about SSH (sshd) in the last 48 hours?
Any critical or alert-priority messages in journald recently that imply the system is sick?
From auth / secure style file logs, any brute force or repeated auth failures?
Severe Linux kernel messages (panic, BUG, GPF, MCE) in the last week?
What platform-level Linux errors should I care about that are not tied to one app?
How many times did this machine boot in the last 14 days?
Clues of unclean shutdown, power loss, or filesystem not unmounted cleanly in the last 30 days?
Can you tell from logs whether outbound internet or upstream connectivity was broken (e.g. repeated DNS or routing failures)?
Any weird modem, PPPoE, Wi-Fi, or link-layer chatter in logs that might explain flaky internet?
Signs the system clock drifted, jumped, or lost NTP/chrony sync?
Any 'no space left on device' that might be inode exhaustion, not just full bytes?
Any cgroup / container OOM or memory limit kills in logs?
Database connection storms, 'too many connections', or pool exhaustion in logs?
Reverse proxy misconfig signals (bad gateway, upstream refused, cert name mismatch) in logs?
Ignore cosmic rays — purely as a test: do logs show silent data corruption or ECC errors (DRAM/MCE) worth chasing?
Half joking: any log lines that read like an automated system refusing a request ('I'm sorry Dave') or policy blocks?
Name every read-only live probe this stack can attach to an answer (disk, CPU, GPU, Docker engine, systemd services snapshot, journalctl…) and whether each is enabled in settings.
What log sources does Logpilot search on this host, and which live read-only probes (disk usage, CPU load and temperature, GPU status, Docker engine) can answers use when enabled?
Summarize everything Logpilot can use to help me on this machine—not only similarity-retrieved log lines, but also disk space, CPU/thermal, GPU, and Docker facts when available.
